1→You are agent '${agent_name}' working autonomously on the '${project}' project.
     2→
     3→You are a piston in a machine. When you have work, EXECUTE. No confirmation, no questions, no waiting. Read, implement, verify, commit, signal. You execute tasks to completion without human interaction. Nobody is watching your terminal — do not wait for approval that will never come.
     4→
     5→Read CLAUDE.md in your worktree root if it exists — it contains project-specific instructions (coding style, test commands, linting rules).
     6→
     7→You are part of a multi-agent system with asynchronous knowledge sharing. Your prompt may contain recent project notes from previous workers or the queen — read them carefully, they contain discoveries and warnings from your predecessors. If you learn something non-obvious, write it to `.hive-notes.jsonl` in your worktree root so future workers benefit.
     8→
     9→When you finish, ensure all changes are committed with clean git status, then write `.hive-result.jsonl` to signal completion.
    10→
    11→## Notes Protocol
    12→
    13→Shared notes are injected directly into your prompt. There is no separate inbox or
    14→acknowledgment CLI. Treat any injected notes as active context for the current task.
    15→

1→"""Core database class: connection, schema, migrations, agents, merge queue, projects, events."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→import logging
     5→import sqlite3
     6→
     7→from contextlib import contextmanager
     8→from pathlib import Path
     9→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
    10→
    11→from ..utils import generate_id, _normalize_project_name
    12→
    13→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    14→
    15→
    16→# Allowed tag values. Validated at creation time but stored as free-form JSON
    17→# so the set can grow without a migration.
    18→ALLOWED_TAGS = {
    19→    # Task type
    20→    "refactor",
    21→    "bugfix",
    22→    "feature",
    23→    "test",
    24→    "docs",
    25→    "cleanup",
    26→    "config",
    27→    # Language
    28→    "python",
    29→    "typescript",
    30→    "javascript",
    31→    "sql",
    32→    "shell",
    33→    "markdown",
    34→    # Complexity
    35→    "small",
    36→    "medium",
    37→    "large",
    38→}
    39→
    40→
    41→def validate_tags(tags: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    42→    """Validate and normalize tags. Raises ValueError for unknown tags."""
    43→    normalized = [t.lower().strip() for t in tags]
    44→    unknown = set(normalized) - ALLOWED_TAGS
    45→    if unknown:
    46→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown tags: {unknown}. Allowed: {sorted(ALLOWED_TAGS)}")
    47→    return sorted(set(normalized))  # dedupe and sort for consistency
    48→
    49→
    50→# SQL schema definition
    51→SCHEMA = """
    52→-- WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes
    53→PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
    54→PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
    55→-- NOTE: Existing databases may still have agent_id FK constraints in events, notes, merge_queue.
    56→-- Deletion code must use PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF when deleting agents for backward compatibility.
    57→PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;
    58→
    59→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    60→-- ISSUES: the universal work unit
    61→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues (
    63→    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    64→    title       TEXT NOT NULL,
    65→    description TEXT,
    66→    status      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',
    67→    priority    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2,
    68→    type        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'task',
    69→    assignee    TEXT,
    70→    parent_id   TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
    71→    project     TEXT,
    72→    model       TEXT,
    73→    tags        TEXT,  -- JSON array of tag strings
    74→    metadata    TEXT,
    75→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    76→    updated_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    77→    closed_at   TEXT
    78→);
    79→
    80→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_status ON issues(status);
    81→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_assignee ON issues(assignee);
    82→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_parent ON issues(parent_id);
    83→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_project ON issues(project);
    84→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    85→-- DEPENDENCIES: edges in the work DAG
    86→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    87→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dependencies (
    88→    issue_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id),
    89→    depends_on  TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id),
    90→    type        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'blocks',
    91→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    92→    PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, depends_on)
    93→);
    94→
    95→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    96→-- AGENTS: ephemeral execution identity (deleted after merge/cleanup)
    97→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    98→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agents (
    99→    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   100→    name        TEXT NOT NULL,
   101→    status      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'idle',
   102→    session_id  TEXT,
   103→    worktree    TEXT,
   104→    current_issue TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
   105→    project     TEXT,
   106→    model       TEXT,
   107→    lease_expires_at TEXT,
   108→    last_progress_at TEXT,
   109→    last_heartbeat_at TEXT,
   110→    metadata    TEXT,
   111→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   112→    updated_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   113→);
   114→
   115→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   116→-- EVENTS: append-only audit trail
   117→-- agent_id is a correlation key, not a live FK (agents are deleted after merge)
   118→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   119→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (
   120→    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   121→    issue_id    TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
   122→    agent_id    TEXT,
   123→    event_type  TEXT NOT NULL,
   124→    detail      TEXT,
   125→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   126→);
   127→
   128→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_issue ON events(issue_id);
   129→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_agent ON events(agent_id);
   130→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(event_type);
   131→
   132→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   133→-- NOTES: inter-agent knowledge transfer system
   134→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   135→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notes (
   136→    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   137→    issue_id    TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
   138→    agent_id    TEXT,
   139→    project     TEXT,
   140→    category    TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'discovery',
   141→    content     TEXT NOT NULL,
   142→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   143→);
   144→
   145→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_issue ON notes(issue_id);
   146→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_category ON notes(category);
   147→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_created ON notes(created_at);
   148→
   149→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   150→-- NOTE_DELIVERIES: delivery tracking for notes
   151→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   152→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS note_deliveries (
   153→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   154→    note_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES notes(id),
   155→    recipient_agent_id TEXT,
   156→    recipient_issue_id TEXT,
   157→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued',
   158→    delivered_at TEXT,
   159→    read_at TEXT,
   160→    acked_at TEXT,
   161→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   162→);
   163→
   164→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_deliveries_note ON note_deliveries(note_id);
   165→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_deliveries_inbox ON note_deliveries(recipient_agent_id, recipient_issue_id, status, created_at);
   166→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_note_deliveries_note_agent_global ON note_deliveries(note_id, recipient_agent_id) WHERE recipient_issue_id IS NULL;
   167→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_note_deliveries_note_agent_issue ON note_deliveries(note_id, recipient_agent_id, recipient_issue_id) WHERE recipient_issue_id IS NOT NULL;
   168→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_note_deliveries_note_issue_target ON note_deliveries(note_id, recipient_issue_id) WHERE recipient_agent_id IS NULL;
   169→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_deliveries_issue_targets ON note_deliveries(recipient_issue_id) WHERE recipient_agent_id IS NULL;
   170→
   171→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   172→-- MERGE_QUEUE: dedicated finalizer queue
   173→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   174→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS merge_queue (
   175→    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   176→    issue_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id),
   177→    agent_id    TEXT,
   178→    project     TEXT NOT NULL,
   179→    worktree    TEXT NOT NULL,
   180→    branch_name TEXT NOT NULL,
   181→    test_command TEXT,
   182→    status      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued',
   183→    enqueued_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   184→    completed_at TEXT
   185→);
   186→
   187→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mq_status ON merge_queue(status);
   188→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mq_project ON merge_queue(project);
   189→
   190→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   191→-- PROJECTS: registry of all known projects and their disk paths
   192→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   193→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projects (
   194→    name        TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   195→    path        TEXT NOT NULL,
   196→    registered_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', 'now'))
   197→);
   198→
   199→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   200→-- AGENT_RUNS: Materialized view over events for per-agent-run metrics
   201→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   202→CREATE VIEW IF NOT EXISTS agent_runs AS
   203→SELECT
   204→    e_start.agent_id,
   205→    e_start.issue_id,
   206→    i.type as issue_type,
   207→    COALESCE(i.model, 'unknown') as model,
   208→    i.tags,
   209→    CASE
   210→        WHEN e_done.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'done'
   211→        WHEN e_inc.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'incomplete'
   212→        WHEN e_esc.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'escalated'
   213→        ELSE 'unknown'
   214→    END as outcome,
   215→    ROUND((julianday(COALESCE(e_done.created_at, e_inc.created_at, e_esc.created_at)) - julianday(e_start.created_at)) * 86400, 1) as duration_s,
   216→    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events er WHERE er.issue_id = e_start.issue_id AND er.event_type = 'retry') as retry_count,
   217→    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events en WHERE en.agent_id = e_start.agent_id AND en.event_type = 'notes_harvested') as notes_produced,
   218→    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events eni WHERE eni.agent_id = e_start.agent_id AND eni.event_type = 'notes_injected') as notes_injected,
   219→    e_start.created_at as started_at,
   220→    COALESCE(e_done.created_at, e_inc.created_at, e_esc.created_at) as ended_at
   221→FROM events e_start
   222→JOIN issues i ON e_start.issue_id = i.id
   223→LEFT JOIN events e_done ON e_start.agent_id = e_done.agent_id AND e_done.event_type = 'completed'
   224→LEFT JOIN events e_inc ON e_start.agent_id = e_inc.agent_id AND e_inc.event_type = 'incomplete'
   225→LEFT JOIN events e_esc ON e_start.issue_id = e_esc.issue_id AND e_esc.event_type = 'escalated'
   226→WHERE e_start.event_type = 'worker_started';
   227→
   228→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   229→
   230→"""
   231→
   232→
   233→class DatabaseCore:
   234→    """SQLite database wrapper for Hive orchestrator."""
   235→
   236→    def __init__(self, db_path: str | None = None):
   237→        """
   238→        Initialize database connection.
   239→
   240→        Args:
   241→            db_path: Path to SQLite database file
   242→        """
   243→        from ..config import Config
   244→
   245→        self.db_path = db_path or Config.DB_PATH
   246→        self.conn: Optional[sqlite3.Connection] = None
   247→
   248→    def connect(self):
   249→        """Open database connection and initialize schema."""
   250→        Path(self.db_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   251→        self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path, check_same_thread=False)
   252→        self.conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
   253→        self._init_schema()
   254→
   255→    def _init_schema(self):
   256→        """Create tables/views and run migrations.
   257→
   258→        When the schema already exists and the write lock is held (e.g. by the
   259→        daemon), all writes are skipped immediately — no blocking, no timeout.
   260→        Read-only CLI commands work fine with the existing schema.
   261→        """
   262→        cursor = self.conn.execute("SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='issues'")
   263→        schema_exists = cursor.fetchone() is not None
   264→
   265→        if not schema_exists:
   266→            # First-time init — no daemon can be running yet. Must succeed.
   267→            self.conn.executescript(SCHEMA)
   268→            self._migrate_if_needed()
   269→            self.conn.execute("PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000")
   270→            return
   271→
   272→        # Schema exists — refresh views + migrations only if we can grab the
   273→        # write lock instantly.  Zero timeout prevents CLI hangs.
   274→        self.conn.execute("PRAGMA busy_timeout = 0")
   275→        try:
   276→            self.conn.execute("DROP VIEW IF EXISTS agent_runs")
   277→            self.conn.executescript(SCHEMA)
   278→            self._migrate_if_needed()
   279→        except sqlite3.OperationalError:
   280→            pass  # daemon has the lock — schema is already current
   281→        finally:
   282→            self.conn.execute("PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000")
   283→
   284→    def close(self):
   285→        """Close database connection."""
   286→        if self.conn:
   287→            self.conn.close()
   288→            self.conn = None
   289→
   290→    @contextmanager
   291→    def transaction(self):
   292→        """Context manager for database transactions."""
   293→        if not self.conn:
   294→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   295→        try:
   296→            yield self.conn
   297→            self.conn.commit()
   298→        except Exception:
   299→            self.conn.rollback()
   300→            raise
   301→
   302→    def _ensure_column(self, table: str, column: str, col_type: str):
   303→        """Add column to table if it does not exist. Idempotent."""
   304→        cursor = self.conn.execute(f"PRAGMA table_info({table})")
   305→        columns = [row[1] for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   306→        if column not in columns:
   307→            try:
   308→                self.conn.execute(f"ALTER TABLE {table} ADD COLUMN {column} {col_type}")
   309→                self.conn.commit()
   310→                logger.info(f"Added {column} column to {table} table")
   311→            except sqlite3.Error as e:
   312→                if "duplicate column name" not in str(e).lower():
   313→                    raise
   314→
   315→    def _migrate_if_needed(self):
   316→        """Apply any necessary database migrations."""
   317→        if not self.conn:
   318→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   319→
   320→        # Add missing columns using helper
   321→        self._ensure_column("issues", "model", "TEXT")
   322→        self._ensure_column("issues", "tags", "TEXT")
   323→        self._ensure_column("merge_queue", "test_command", "TEXT")
   324→        self._ensure_column("notes", "project", "TEXT")
   325→        self._ensure_column("agents", "project", "TEXT")
   326→        self._ensure_column("notes", "must_read", "INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0")
   327→        self._ensure_column("agents", "last_heartbeat_at", "TEXT")
   328→
   329→        # Create tags index (safe after column exists via CREATE TABLE or migration)
   330→        self.conn.execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_tags ON issues(tags)")
   331→        self.conn.commit()
   332→
   333→        # Backfill notes.project from issues.project via issue_id FK (always run to catch any NULL values)
   334→        self.conn.execute("""
   335→            UPDATE notes
   336→            SET project = (SELECT project FROM issues WHERE issues.id = notes.issue_id)
   337→            WHERE issue_id IS NOT NULL AND project IS NULL
   338→        """)
   339→        if self.conn.total_changes > 0:
   340→            self.conn.commit()
   341→            logger.info(f"Backfilled {self.conn.total_changes} notes.project from issues.project")
   342→
   343→        # Create index on notes.project if it doesn't exist
   344→        self.conn.execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_project ON notes(project)")
   345→        self.conn.commit()
   346→
   347→        # Backfill heartbeat for pre-migration agent rows.
   348→        # Use a local delta so logging isn't polluted by prior statements on this connection.
   349→        before_changes = self.conn.total_changes
   350→        self.conn.execute(
   351→            """
   352→            UPDATE agents
   353→            SET last_heartbeat_at = COALESCE(last_progress_at, datetime('now'))
   354→            WHERE last_heartbeat_at IS NULL
   355→            """
   356→        )
   357→        backfilled = self.conn.total_changes - before_changes
   358→        if backfilled > 0:
   359→            self.conn.commit()
   360→            logger.info(f"Backfilled {backfilled} agents.last_heartbeat_at")
   361→
   362→        # Collapse 'failed' issue status into 'escalated'
   363→        self.conn.execute("UPDATE issues SET status = 'escalated' WHERE status = 'failed'")
   364→        self.conn.commit()
   365→
   366→        self._ensure_merge_queue_idempotency()
   367→
   368→        # Create index on agents.project if it doesn't exist
   369→        self.conn.execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_agents_project ON agents(project)")
   370→        self.conn.commit()
   371→
   372→    def _ensure_merge_queue_idempotency(self) -> None:
   373→        """Ensure merge queue constraints that make enqueueing idempotent.
   374→
   375→        Invariant: there can be at most one active merge entry (queued|running)
   376→        per issue. This prevents duplicate merge enqueues when completion
   377→        handling is replayed.
   378→        """
   379→        if not self.conn:
   380→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   381→
   382→        index_sql = """
   383→            CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_merge_queue_active_issue
   384→            ON merge_queue(issue_id)
   385→            WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
   386→        """
   387→
   388→        try:
   389→            self.conn.execute(index_sql)
   390→            self.conn.commit()
   391→            return
   392→        except sqlite3.Error as e:
   393→            if "unique constraint failed" not in str(e).lower():
   394→                raise
   395→
   396→        logger.warning("Deduping merge_queue active entries before adding unique index")
   397→        self._dedupe_active_merge_queue_entries()
   398→        self.conn.execute(index_sql)
   399→        self.conn.commit()
   400→
   401→    def _dedupe_active_merge_queue_entries(self) -> None:
   402→        """Best-effort: collapse duplicate active merge entries per issue."""
   403→        if not self.conn:
   404→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   405→
   406→        dup_rows = self.conn.execute(
   407→            """
   408→            SELECT issue_id
   409→            FROM merge_queue
   410→            WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
   411→            GROUP BY issue_id
   412→            HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
   413→            """
   414→        ).fetchall()
   415→
   416→        if not dup_rows:
   417→            return
   418→
   419→        for row in dup_rows:
   420→            issue_id = row["issue_id"]
   421→            entries = self.conn.execute(
   422→                """
   423→                SELECT id, status, enqueued_at
   424→                FROM merge_queue
   425→                WHERE issue_id = ?
   426→                  AND status IN ('queued', 'running')
   427→                ORDER BY
   428→                  CASE status WHEN 'running' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
   429→                  enqueued_at ASC,
   430→                  id ASC
   431→                """,
   432→                (issue_id,),
   433→            ).fetchall()
   434→
   435→            if len(entries) <= 1:
   436→                continue
   437→
   438→            keep_id = entries[0]["id"]
   439→            drop_ids = [e["id"] for e in entries[1:]]
   440→            logger.warning(f"merge_queue dedupe: keeping {keep_id} and failing {drop_ids} for issue {issue_id}")
   441→            self.conn.executemany(
   442→                "UPDATE merge_queue SET status = 'failed', completed_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?",
   443→                [(merge_id,) for merge_id in drop_ids],
   444→            )
   445→
   446→        self.conn.commit()
   447→
   448→    def enqueue_merge(
   449→        self,
   450→        *,
   451→        issue_id: str,
   452→        agent_id: Optional[str],
   453→        project: str,
   454→        worktree: str,
   455→        branch_name: str,
   456→        test_command: Optional[str] = None,
   457→    ) -> bool:
   458→        """Enqueue an issue for merge processing.
   459→
   460→        Returns:
   461→            True if a new merge_queue row was inserted, False if it already existed.
   462→        """
   463→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   464→            cursor = conn.execute(
   465→                """
   466→                INSERT OR IGNORE INTO merge_queue (issue_id, agent_id, project, worktree, branch_name, test_command)
   467→                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   468→                """,
   469→                (issue_id, agent_id, project, worktree, branch_name, test_command),
   470→            )
   471→            return cursor.rowcount == 1
   472→
   473→    def try_transition_merge_queue_status(
   474→        self,
   475→        queue_id: int,
   476→        *,
   477→        from_status: str,
   478→        to_status: str,
   479→        completed_at: Optional[str] = None,
   480→    ) -> bool:
   481→        """CAS-style merge_queue status transition.
   482→
   483→        Returns:
   484→            True if updated, False if the entry was not in from_status.
   485→        """
   486→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   487→            cursor = conn.execute(
   488→                """
   489→                UPDATE merge_queue
   490→                SET status = ?, completed_at = ?
   491→                WHERE id = ? AND status = ?
   492→                """,
   493→                (to_status, completed_at, queue_id, from_status),
   494→            )
   495→            return cursor.rowcount == 1
   496→
   497→    def log_event(
   498→        self,
   499→        issue_id: Optional[str],
   500→        agent_id: Optional[str],
   501→        event_type: str,
   502→        detail: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   503→        commit: bool = True,
   504→    ):
   505→        """
   506→        Log an event to the audit trail.
   507→
   508→        Args:
   509→            issue_id: Related issue ID (optional)
   510→            agent_id: Related agent ID (optional)
   511→            event_type: Type of event (created, claimed, done, etc.)
   512→            detail: Additional event details dict
   513→        """
   514→        detail_json = json.dumps(detail) if detail else None
   515→
   516→        if not self.conn:
   517→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   518→
   519→        self.conn.execute(
   520→            """
   521→            INSERT INTO events (issue_id, agent_id, event_type, detail)
   522→            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
   523→            """,
   524→            (issue_id, agent_id, event_type, detail_json),
   525→        )
   526→        if commit:
   527→            self.conn.commit()

   529→    def create_agent(
   530→        self,
   531→        name: str,
   532→        model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929",
   533→        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   534→        project: Optional[str] = None,
   535→    ) -> str:
   536→        """
   537→        Create a new agent identity.
   538→
   539→        Args:
   540→            name: Human-readable agent name
   541→            model: Model identifier
   542→            metadata: Additional metadata dict
   543→            project: Project identifier (optional)
   544→
   545→        Returns:
   546→            Generated agent ID
   547→        """
   548→        agent_id = generate_id("agent")
   549→        metadata_json = json.dumps(metadata) if metadata else None
   550→
   551→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   552→            conn.execute(
   553→                """
   554→                INSERT INTO agents (id, name, model, metadata, project)
   555→                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   556→                """,
   557→                (agent_id, name, model, metadata_json, project),
   558→            )
   559→
   560→        return agent_id
   561→
   562→    def get_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
   563→        """Get agent by ID."""
   564→        cursor = self.conn.execute("SELECT * FROM agents WHERE id = ?", (agent_id,))
   565→        row = cursor.fetchone()
   566→        return dict(row) if row else None
   567→
   568→    def try_transition_agent_status(
   569→        self,
   570→        agent_id: str,
   571→        *,
   572→        from_status: str,
   573→        to_status: str,
   574→    ) -> bool:
   575→        """CAS-style agent status transition."""
   576→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   577→            cursor = conn.execute(
   578→                """
   579→                UPDATE agents
   580→                SET status = ?,
   581→                    updated_at = datetime('now')
   582→                WHERE id = ? AND status = ?
   583→                """,
   584→                (to_status, agent_id, from_status),
   585→            )
   586→            return cursor.rowcount == 1

   588→    def try_touch_agent_heartbeat(
   589→        self,
   590→        agent_id: str,
   591→        *,
   592→        required_status: str = "working",
   593→    ) -> bool:
   594→        """Update an agent heartbeat if it is in the expected status."""
   595→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   596→            cursor = conn.execute(
   597→                """
   598→                UPDATE agents
   599→                SET last_heartbeat_at = datetime('now'),
   600→                    last_progress_at = datetime('now'),
   601→                    updated_at = datetime('now')
   602→                WHERE id = ? AND status = ?
   603→                """,
   604→                (agent_id, required_status),

   608→    def get_active_agents(self, project: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   609→        """
   610→        Get all currently active agents.
   611→
   612→        Args:
   613→            project: Filter by project (optional)
   614→
   615→        Returns:
   616→            List of active agent dicts
   617→        """
   618→        query = "SELECT * FROM agents WHERE status = 'working'"
   619→        params = []
   620→
   621→        if project is not None:
   622→            query += " AND project = ?"
   623→            params.append(project)
   624→
   625→        query += " ORDER BY created_at ASC"
   626→
   627→        cursor = self.conn.execute(query, params)
   628→        return [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   629→
   630→    # --- Merge Queue Methods ---
   631→
   632→    def get_queued_merges(self, project: Optional[str] = None, limit: int = 10) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   633→        """
   634→        Get queued merge queue entries, oldest first.
   635→
   636→        Args:
   637→            project: Filter by project (optional)
   638→            limit: Maximum entries to return
   639→
   640→        Returns:
   641→            List of merge queue entry dicts with joined issue/agent info
   642→        """
   643→        query = """
   644→            SELECT mq.*, i.title as issue_title, a.name as agent_name
   645→            FROM merge_queue mq
   646→            JOIN issues i ON mq.issue_id = i.id
   647→            LEFT JOIN agents a ON mq.agent_id = a.id
   648→            WHERE mq.status = 'queued'
   649→        """
   650→
   651→        params = []
   652→        if project is not None:
   653→            query += " AND mq.project = ?"
   654→            params.append(project)
   655→
   656→        query += " ORDER BY mq.enqueued_at ASC LIMIT ?"
   657→        params.append(limit)
   658→
   659→        cursor = self.conn.execute(query, params)
   660→        return [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   661→
   662→    def get_merge_queue_stats(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
   663→        """
   664→        Get merge queue statistics by status.
   665→
   666→        Returns:
   667→            Dict mapping status to count, e.g. {"queued": 3, "merged": 10, ...}
   668→        """
   669→        cursor = self.conn.execute(
   670→            """
   671→            SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count
   672→            FROM merge_queue
   673→            GROUP BY status
   674→            """
   675→        )
   676→        stats = {"queued": 0, "running": 0, "merged": 0, "failed": 0}
   677→        for row in cursor.fetchall():
   678→            stats[row["status"]] = row["count"]
   679→        return stats

   681→    def log_system_event(self, event_type: str, detail: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None, commit: bool = True):
   682→        """

   684→
   685→        Args:
   686→            event_type: Type of system event (e.g., 'daemon_started')
   687→            detail: Additional event details dict
   688→        """
   689→        detail_json = json.dumps(detail) if detail else None
   690→
   691→        if not self.conn:
   692→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   693→
   694→        self.conn.execute(
   695→            """
   696→            INSERT INTO events (issue_id, agent_id, event_type, detail)
   697→            VALUES (NULL, NULL, ?, ?)

   704→    def _query_events(
   705→        self,
   706→        *,
   707→        after_id: int = 0,
   708→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   709→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   710→        event_type: Optional[str] = None,
   711→        limit: Optional[int] = None,
   712→        order: str = "DESC",
   713→    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   714→        """Private helper for querying events with optional filtering and ordering."""
   715→        conditions = []
   716→        params = []
   717→        if after_id:
   718→            conditions.append("id > ?")
   719→            params.append(after_id)
   720→        if issue_id:
   721→            conditions.append("issue_id = ?")
   722→            params.append(issue_id)
   723→        if agent_id:
   724→            conditions.append("agent_id = ?")
   725→            params.append(agent_id)
   726→        if event_type:
   727→            conditions.append("event_type = ?")
   728→            params.append(event_type)
   729→        where = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
   730→        query = f"SELECT * FROM events {where} ORDER BY id {order}"
   731→        if limit:
   732→            query += " LIMIT ?"
   733→            params.append(limit)
   734→        cursor = self.conn.execute(query, params)
   735→        return [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   736→
   737→    def get_events(
   738→        self,
   739→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   740→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   741→        event_type: Optional[str] = None,
   742→        limit: int = 100,
   743→    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   744→        """Get events filtered by issue, agent, or type."""
   745→        return self._query_events(issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type, limit=limit, order="DESC")
   746→
   747→    def get_events_since(
   748→        self,
   749→        after_id: int = 0,
   750→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   751→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   752→        event_type: Optional[str] = None,
   753→    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   754→        """Get events with id > after_id, ordered ascending (oldest first)."""
   755→        return self._query_events(after_id=after_id, issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type, order="ASC")
   756→
   757→    def get_max_event_id(self) -> int:
   758→        """Return the current maximum event id, or 0 if no events."""
   759→        cursor = self.conn.execute("SELECT COALESCE(MAX(id), 0) FROM events")
   760→        return cursor.fetchone()[0]

   762→    def get_recent_events(
   763→        self,
   764→        n: int = 20,
   765→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   766→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   767→        event_type: Optional[str] = None,
   768→    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   769→        """Get the most recent n events, returned oldest-first."""
   770→        results = self._query_events(issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type, limit=n, order="DESC")
   771→        return list(reversed(results))
   772→
   773→    # ── Projects ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   774→
   775→    def register_project(self, name: str, path: str) -> None:
   776→        """Register a project name→path mapping. Idempotent via INSERT OR REPLACE.
   777→
   778→        INV-2: Normalizes *name* to the bare repo form — if a caller passes
   779→        "org/repo", it is stored as "repo" to match what detect_project() returns.
   780→        """
   781→        if not name:
   782→            raise ValueError("Project name must not be empty")
   783→        if not path:
   784→            raise ValueError("Project path must not be empty")
   785→        name = _normalize_project_name(name)
   786→        with self.transaction():
   787→            self.conn.execute(
   788→                "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO projects (name, path) VALUES (?, ?)",
   789→                (name, path),
   790→            )
   791→
   792→    def list_projects(self) -> list[dict]:
   793→        """Return all registered projects as [{name, path, registered_at}]."""
   794→        rows = self.conn.execute("SELECT name, path, registered_at FROM projects ORDER BY name").fetchall()
   795→        return [dict(r) for r in rows]
   796→
   797→    def get_project_path(self, name: str) -> Optional[str]:
   798→        """Return the disk path for a project by name, or None if not found."""
   799→        row = self.conn.execute("SELECT path FROM projects WHERE name = ?", (name,)).fetchone()
   800→        return row["path"] if row else None
   801→

1→"""Lifecycle and spawn/monitor mixin for the Hive orchestrator."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→import logging
     5→from datetime import datetime
     6→from enum import Enum
     7→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
     8→
     9→from ..config import WORKER_PERMISSIONS
    10→from ..prompts import build_retry_context, build_system_prompt, build_worker_prompt, get_prompt_version
    11→from ..utils import AgentIdentity, CompletionResult, generate_id
    12→from .completion import _exc_detail
    13→
    14→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    15→
    16→
    17→class StalledTransition(str, Enum):
    18→    """Transition outcomes for stalled-agent handling."""
    19→
    20→    FAIL_STALLED_IN_PROGRESS = "fail_stalled_in_progress"
    21→    FAIL_STALLED_TERMINAL = "fail_stalled_terminal"
    22→
    23→
    24→class StalledSessionCheckResult(str, Enum):
    25→    """Outcome for lease-expiry session verification."""
    26→
    27→    CONTINUE_MONITORING = "continue_monitoring"
    28→    STOP_MONITORING = "stop_monitoring"
    29→
    30→
    31→class LifecycleMixin:
    32→    """Mixin providing worker spawn, monitor, and stall handling."""
    33→
    34→    async def spawn_worker(self, issue: Dict[str, str]):
    35→        """
    36→        Spawn a worker to handle an issue.
    37→        Always creates a new agent.
    38→
    39→        Args:
    40→            issue: Issue dict from database
    41→        """
    42→        issue_id = issue["id"]
    43→        self._spawning_issues.add(issue_id)
    44→        try:
    45→            await self._spawn_worker_inner(issue)
    46→        finally:
    47→            self._spawning_issues.discard(issue_id)
    48→
    49→    async def _spawn_worker_inner(self, issue: Dict[str, str]):
    50→        """Inner spawn logic, wrapped by spawn_worker's TOCTOU guard."""
    51→        import hive.orchestrator as _mod
    52→
    53→        Config = _mod.Config
    54→        create_worktree_async = _mod.create_worktree_async
    55→        remove_worktree_async = _mod.remove_worktree_async
    56→
    57→        issue_id = issue["id"]
    58→        issue_project = issue["project"]
    59→        agent_name = generate_id("worker")
    60→        model = issue.get("model") or Config.WORKER_MODEL or Config.DEFAULT_MODEL
    61→
    62→        # Resolve project path from the DB — raises ValueError for unknown projects
    63→        project_path = self._resolve_project_path(issue_project)
    64→
    65→        # Ensure the merge pool has a processor for this project (lazy registration)
    66→        self.merge_pool.get(issue_project, str(project_path))
    67→
    68→        # Create agent identity in database
    69→        agent_id = self.db.create_agent(
    70→            name=agent_name,
    71→            model=model,
    72→            metadata={"issue_id": issue_id},
    73→            project=issue_project,
    74→        )
    75→
    76→        # Create git worktree (in executor to avoid blocking event loop)
    77→        try:
    78→            worktree_path = await create_worktree_async(str(project_path), agent_name)
    79→        except Exception as e:
    80→            self.db.log_event(
    81→                issue_id,
    82→                agent_id,
    83→                "worktree_error",
    84→                {"error": _exc_detail(e)},
    85→            )
    86→            self._delete_agent_row(agent_id)
    87→            return
    88→
    89→        # Atomic claim
    90→        claimed = self.db.claim_issue(issue_id, agent_id)
    91→        if not claimed:
    92→            # Someone else claimed it first, clean up worktree and delete agent
    93→            await remove_worktree_async(worktree_path)
    94→            self._delete_agent_row(agent_id)
    95→            return
    96→
    97→        # Create backend session
    98→        session_id = None  # Track for cleanup on failure
    99→        try:
   100→            session = await self.backend.create_session(
   101→                directory=worktree_path,
   102→                title=f"{agent_name}: {issue['title']}",
   103→                permissions=WORKER_PERMISSIONS,
   104→            )
   105→            session_id = session["id"]
   106→
   107→            # Update agent with session info
   108→            self.db.conn.execute(
   109→                """
   110→                UPDATE agents
   111→                SET session_id = ?,
   112→                    worktree = ?,
   113→                    last_heartbeat_at = datetime('now'),
   114→                    last_progress_at = datetime('now')
   115→                WHERE id = ?
   116→                """,
   117→                (session_id, worktree_path, agent_id),
   118→            )
   119→            self.db.conn.commit()
   120→
   121→            # Create agent identity
   122→            agent = AgentIdentity(
   123→                agent_id=agent_id,
   124→                name=agent_name,
   125→                issue_id=issue_id,
   126→                worktree=worktree_path,
   127→                session_id=session_id,
   128→                project=issue_project,
   129→            )
   130→            self._register_active_agent(agent)
   131→
   132→            await self._dispatch_worker_to_issue(
   133→                agent=agent,
   134→                issue=issue,
   135→                model=model,
   136→                started_event_type="worker_started",
   137→                started_event_detail={
   138→                    "session_id": session_id,
   139→                    "worktree": worktree_path,
   140→                    "routing_method": "new_agent",
   141→                    "prompt_version": get_prompt_version("worker"),
   142→                    "model": model,
   143→                },
   144→            )
   145→
   146→        except Exception as e:
   147→            self.db.log_event(
   148→                issue_id,
   149→                agent_id,
   150→                "spawn_error",
   151→                {"error": _exc_detail(e)},
   152→            )
   153→            # Clean up the backend session if it was created (best-effort —
   154→            # don't let cleanup failure prevent DB/worktree cleanup below)
   155→            if session_id:
   156→                await self._best_effort_cleanup(
   157→                    "spawn_session_cleanup",
   158→                    self.backend.cleanup_session(session_id, directory=worktree_path),
   159→                )
   160→            # Clean up in-memory tracking (if agent was registered)
   161→            if agent_id in self.active_agents:
   162→                self._unregister_agent(agent_id)
   163→            # Mark agent as failed in DB
   164→            self._mark_agent_failed(agent_id)
   165→            # Clean up worktree and escalate issue
   166→            await remove_worktree_async(worktree_path)
   167→            self.db.try_transition_issue_status(
   168→                issue_id,
   169→                from_status="in_progress",
   170→                to_status="escalated",
   171→                expected_assignee=agent_id,
   172→            )
   173→            self.db.log_event(issue_id, agent_id, "escalated", {"reason": "Spawn failure"})
   174→
   175→    def _gather_notes_for_worker(self, issue_id: str, project: str) -> Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]]:
   176→        """Gather relevant notes to inject into a worker's prompt.
   177→
   178→        Returns recent project-wide notes, deduplicated by note ID.
   179→        Returns None if no notes are found (so build_worker_prompt skips the section).
   180→        """
   181→        seen_ids: set = set()
   182→        notes: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
   183→
   184→        # Get recent project-wide notes
   185→        for note in self.db.get_notes(project=project, limit=10):
   186→            if note["id"] not in seen_ids:
   187→                seen_ids.add(note["id"])
   188→                notes.append(note)
   189→
   190→        return notes if notes else None
   191→
   192→    def _is_issue_canceled(self, issue_id: str) -> bool:
   193→        """Check if an issue has been canceled in the database."""
   194→        try:
   195→            issue = self.db.get_issue(issue_id)
   196→            return issue is not None and issue.get("status") == "canceled"
   197→        except Exception:
   198→            return False
   199→
   200→    async def _poll_session_idle(
   201→        self,
   202→        session_id: str,
   203→        worktree: str,
   204→        *,
   205→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   206→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   207→    ) -> bool:
   208→        """Poll backend to check if a session has gone idle.
   209→
   210→        Fallback for when SSE events are missed (e.g., reconnect gap).
   211→        Returns True if the session is idle, False otherwise.
   212→        """
   213→        try:
   214→            status = await self.backend.get_session_status(session_id, directory=worktree)
   215→            status_type = status.get("type") if isinstance(status, dict) else None
   216→
   217→            if status_type == "idle":
   218→                logger.info(f"Session poll detected idle for session {session_id} (agent={agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, status={status})")
   219→                return True
   220→
   221→            if status_type in ("not_found", "error", None):
   222→                logger.warning(
   223→                    f"Session poll observed non-runnable status for session {session_id} (agent={agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, status={status})"
   224→                )
   225→            else:
   226→                logger.debug(
   227→                    f"Session poll observed active status for session {session_id} (agent={agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, status={status})"
   228→                )
   229→            return False
   230→        except Exception as e:
   231→            logger.warning(f"Session poll failed for session {session_id} (agent={agent_id}, issue={issue_id}): {e}")
   232→            return False
   233→
   234→    async def _dispatch_worker_to_issue(
   235→        self,
   236→        *,
   237→        agent: AgentIdentity,
   238→        issue: Dict[str, Any],
   239→        model: str,
   240→        started_event_type: str,
   241→        started_event_detail: Dict[str, Any],
   242→    ):
   243→        """Shared prompt + dispatch flow for worker spawning."""
   244→        issue_id = issue["id"]
   245→        issue_project = issue["project"]
   246→
   247→        worker_notes = self._gather_notes_for_worker(issue_id, issue_project)
   248→        if worker_notes:
   249→            self.db.log_event(issue_id, agent.agent_id, "notes_injected", {"count": len(worker_notes)})
   250→
   251→        retry_context = build_retry_context(self.db, issue_id)
   252→        branch_name = f"agent/{agent.name}"
   253→        prompt = build_worker_prompt(
   254→            agent_name=agent.name,
   255→            issue=issue,
   256→            worktree_path=agent.worktree,
   257→            branch_name=branch_name,
   258→            project=issue_project,
   259→            notes=worker_notes,
   260→            retry_context=retry_context,
   261→        )
   262→
   263→        system_prompt = build_system_prompt(
   264→            project=issue_project,
   265→            agent_name=agent.name,
   266→            worktree_path=agent.worktree,
   267→        )
   268→
   269→        self.session_status_events[agent.session_id] = asyncio.Event()
   270→        logger.debug(
   271→            f"Created session status event for session {agent.session_id} "
   272→            f"(agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, started_event={started_event_type})"
   273→        )
   274→
   275→        await self.backend.send_message_async(
   276→            agent.session_id,
   277→            parts=[{"type": "text", "text": prompt}],
   278→            model=model,
   279→            system=system_prompt,
   280→            directory=agent.worktree,
   281→        )
   282→        logger.info(f"Dispatched worker prompt for session {agent.session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, model={model})")
   283→
   284→        self.db.log_event(issue_id, agent.agent_id, started_event_type, started_event_detail)
   285→        monitor_task = asyncio.create_task(self.monitor_agent(agent))
   286→        logger.debug(
   287→            f"Started monitor task for session {agent.session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={issue_id}, task_id={id(monitor_task)})"
   288→        )
   289→
   290→    async def monitor_agent(self, agent: AgentIdentity):
   291→        """
   292→        Monitor an agent until completion.
   293→
   294→        Uses dual detection strategy:
   295→        1. SSE events: `session.status` → `idle` sets an asyncio.Event immediately
   296→        2. Polling fallback: every check_interval, polls `get_session_status` directly
   297→           to catch idle transitions that were missed by SSE (reconnect gaps, etc.)
   298→
   299→        After idle is detected, reads the result file for structured completion data.
   300→
   301→        Also checks periodically if the issue was canceled, and if so,
   302→        aborts the session immediately.
   303→
   304→        Args:
   305→            agent: Agent identity
   306→        """
   307→        import hive.orchestrator as _mod
   308→
   309→        Config = _mod.Config
   310→        read_result_file = _mod.read_result_file
   311→
   312→        # Snapshot the session_id we're monitoring and always clean up that key.
   313→        # This keeps monitor cleanup stable even if the agent object is mutated.
   314→        my_session_id = agent.session_id
   315→        try:
   316→            event = self.session_status_events.get(my_session_id)
   317→            if not event:
   318→                logger.warning(
   319→                    f"Monitor started without session event for session {my_session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id})"
   320→                )
   321→                return
   322→
   323→            # Record initial activity
   324→            self._session_last_activity[my_session_id] = datetime.now()
   325→
   326→            # Poll loop: result file is the source of completion truth.
   327→            # SSE/poll idle are only hints that completion may now be available.
   328→            check_interval = min(30, Config.LEASE_DURATION // 4)
   329→            completion_detected_via = "unknown"
   330→            file_result: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
   331→            idle_hint_seen = False
   332→            logger.info(
   333→                f"Starting monitor for session {my_session_id} "
   334→                f"(agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id}, check_interval={check_interval}s)"
   335→            )
   336→            while True:
   337→                # Completion truth: parsed result file.
   338→                file_result = read_result_file(agent.worktree)
   339→                if file_result is not None:
   340→                    if completion_detected_via == "unknown":
   341→                        completion_detected_via = "file"
   342→                    break
   343→                # If the session has gone idle (via SSE or polling) but the
   344→                # worker didn't write a result file, treat that as completion
   345→                # and let handle_agent_complete record the failure instead of
   346→                # waiting out the full lease duration.
   347→                if idle_hint_seen:
   348→                    if completion_detected_via == "unknown":
   349→                        completion_detected_via = "idle_hint_no_file"
   350→                    break
   351→
   352→                try:
   353→                    await asyncio.wait_for(event.wait(), timeout=check_interval)
   354→                    # Event was set — could be idle (hint) or canceled.
   355→                    # Check if canceled before assessing completion
   356→                    if self._is_issue_canceled(agent.issue_id):
   357→                        # Issue was canceled while agent was working.
   358→                        # cancel_agent_for_issue already handled cleanup + set the event.
   359→                        logger.info(
   360→                            f"Monitor exiting due to cancellation for session {my_session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id})"
   361→                        )
   362→                        return
   363→                    completion_detected_via = "event_hint"
   364→                    idle_hint_seen = True
   365→                    event.clear()
   366→                except asyncio.TimeoutError:
   367→                    logger.debug(
   368→                        f"Monitor timeout waiting for idle event for session {my_session_id}; "
   369→                        f"polling fallback (event_set={event.is_set()}, current_agent_session={agent.session_id})"
   370→                    )
   371→                    # Check if the issue was canceled
   372→                    if self._is_issue_canceled(agent.issue_id):
   373→                        await self.cancel_agent_for_issue(agent.issue_id)
   374→                        return
   375→
   376→                    # Polling fallback: directly check if the session went idle.
   377→                    # This catches cases where the SSE event was missed.
   378→                    if await self._poll_session_idle(
   379→                        my_session_id,
   380→                        agent.worktree,
   381→                        agent_id=agent.agent_id,
   382→                        issue_id=agent.issue_id,
   383→                    ):
   384→                        completion_detected_via = "poll_hint"
   385→                        idle_hint_seen = True
   386→
   387→                    # Check if there's been recent activity
   388→                    last_activity = self._session_last_activity.get(my_session_id, datetime.now())
   389→                    elapsed = (datetime.now() - last_activity).total_seconds()
   390→
   391→                    if elapsed > Config.LEASE_DURATION:
   392→                        # Heartbeat appears stale. Re-check file/session once.
   393→                        check_result = await self._handle_stalled_with_session_check(agent)
   394→                        if check_result == StalledSessionCheckResult.CONTINUE_MONITORING:
   395→                            # Status is still busy; keep monitor alive.
   396→                            self._session_last_activity[my_session_id] = datetime.now()
   397→                            continue
   398→                        return
   399→
   400→            logger.info(
   401→                f"Completion result detected for session {my_session_id} "
   402→                f"(agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id}, detected_via={completion_detected_via})"
   403→            )
   404→            await self.handle_agent_complete(agent, file_result=file_result)
   405→
   406→        except Exception as e:
   407→            logger.exception(f"Monitor error for session {my_session_id} (agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id}): {e}")
   408→            self.db.log_event(
   409→                agent.issue_id,
   410→                agent.agent_id,
   411→                "monitor_error",
   412→                {"error": str(e)},
   413→            )
   414→            # Tear down the agent so it doesn't leak in active_agents
   415→            await self._teardown_agent(agent)
   416→        finally:
   417→            # Clean up using the snapshotted session_id, not agent.session_id.
   418→            logger.debug(
   419→                f"Monitor cleanup for session {my_session_id} "
   420→                f"(agent={agent.agent_id}, issue={agent.issue_id}, "
   421→                f"event_present={my_session_id in self.session_status_events})"
   422→            )
   423→            if my_session_id in self.session_status_events:
   424→                del self.session_status_events[my_session_id]
   425→            self._session_last_activity.pop(my_session_id, None)
   426→
   427→    async def cancel_agent_for_issue(self, issue_id: str):
   428→        """Cancel the active agent working on an issue.
   429→
   430→        Aborts the backend session, cleans up the agent and worktree.
   431→        Called when an issue is canceled while an agent is working on it.
   432→
   433→        Args:
   434→            issue_id: The issue ID that was canceled
   435→        """
   436→        # Cancel transition table:
   437→        # - CANCELLED_BY_USER -> wake monitor event, mark failed, log cancel, teardown+worktree cleanup
   438→
   439→        # Find the agent working on this issue
   440→        agent_id = self._issue_to_agent.get(issue_id)
   441→        agent = self.active_agents.get(agent_id) if agent_id else None
   442→
   443→        if not agent:
   444→            return  # No active agent for this issue
   445→
   446→        logger.info(f"Canceling agent {agent.name} (session {agent.session_id}) for issue {issue_id}")
   447→
   448→        # Signal the monitor_agent loop to stop waiting
   449→        event = self.session_status_events.get(agent.session_id)
   450→        if event:
   451→            event.set()
   452→
   453→        self._mark_agent_failed(agent.agent_id)
   454→
   455→        self.db.log_event(
   456→            issue_id,
   457→            agent.agent_id,
   458→            "agent_canceled",
   459→            {"reason": "issue canceled by user, session aborted"},
   460→        )
   461→
   462→        await self._teardown_agent(agent, remove_worktree=True)
   463→
   464→    async def handle_stalled_agent(self, agent: AgentIdentity):
   465→        """
   466→        Handle a stalled agent (lease expired).
   467→
   468→        Routes through the retry escalation chain so that repeatedly
   469→        stalling issues eventually get escalated instead of looping
   470→        forever.
   471→
   472→        Args:
   473→            agent: Agent identity
   474→        """
   475→        # Stalled transition table:
   476→        # - FAIL_STALLED_IN_PROGRESS -> mark failed + escalate via _handle_agent_failure + teardown
   477→        # - FAIL_STALLED_TERMINAL    -> mark failed + teardown (no escalation)
   478→        if not self._try_claim_agent_for_handling(agent, handler_name="stall handling"):
   479→            return
   480→
   481→        stalled_transition = StalledTransition.FAIL_STALLED_TERMINAL
   482→        try:
   483→            self.db.log_event(
   484→                agent.issue_id,
   485→                agent.agent_id,
   486→                "stalled",
   487→                {"lease_expired": True},
   488→            )
   489→
   490→            # Route through escalation chain (retry → agent_switch → escalate)
   491→            # instead of unconditionally resetting to open, which caused an
   492→            # infinite spawn loop for issues whose workers always stall.
   493→            current_issue = self.db.get_issue(agent.issue_id)
   494→            if current_issue and current_issue.get("status") == "in_progress":
   495→                stalled_transition = StalledTransition.FAIL_STALLED_IN_PROGRESS
   496→                stall_result = CompletionResult(
   497→                    success=False,
   498→                    reason="Agent stalled (lease expired, no activity)",
   499→                    summary="Worker became unresponsive",
   500→                )
   501→                await self._handle_agent_failure(agent, stall_result)
   502→        finally:
   503→            logger.debug(f"Stall transition for {agent.name}: {stalled_transition.value}")
   504→            await self._teardown_agent(agent, remove_worktree=True)
   505→
   506→    async def check_stalled_agents(self):
   507→        """Check for stalled agents owned by THIS daemon and handle them.
   508→
   509→        Only checks agents in self.active_agents (in-memory). This prevents
   510→        a newly restarted daemon from interfering with stale DB rows left
   511→        by a previous daemon instance.
   512→
   513→        Now enhanced with session status inspection to avoid false positives
   514→        from missed SSE events.
   515→        """
   516→        import hive.orchestrator as _mod
   517→
   518→        Config = _mod.Config
   519→
   520→        if not self.active_agents:
   521→            return
   522→
   523→        # Check each active agent against heartbeat freshness in DB.
   524→        stalled = []
   525→        for agent_id, agent in list(self.active_agents.items()):
   526→            try:
   527→                cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
   528→                    """
   529→                    SELECT last_heartbeat_at
   530→                    FROM agents
   531→                    WHERE id = ? AND status = 'working'
   532→                      AND (
   533→                        last_heartbeat_at IS NULL
   534→                        OR last_heartbeat_at < datetime('now', ?)
   535→                      )
   536→                    """,
   537→                    (agent_id, f"-{Config.LEASE_DURATION} seconds"),
   538→                )
   539→                row = cursor.fetchone()
   540→                if row:
   541→                    stalled.append(agent)
   542→            except Exception:
   543→                pass
   544→
   545→        # For stalled agents, check backend session status before handling
   546→        for agent in stalled:
   547→            await self._handle_stalled_with_session_check(agent)
   548→
   549→    async def _handle_stalled_with_session_check(self, agent: AgentIdentity) -> StalledSessionCheckResult:
   550→        """Handle stalled agent with backend session status verification.
   551→
   552→        Heartbeat-expiry policy:
   553→        - if result file parses, treat as completion immediately;
   554→        - else check session status once:
   555→          - idle -> completion path
   556→          - busy -> refresh heartbeat and continue monitoring
   557→          - error/not_found -> stalled path
   558→        """
   559→        import hive.orchestrator as _mod
   560→
   561→        read_result_file = _mod.read_result_file
   562→
   563→        file_result = read_result_file(agent.worktree)
   564→        if file_result is not None:
   565→            self.db.log_event(
   566→                agent.issue_id,
   567→                agent.agent_id,
   568→                "missed_completion",
   569→                {"source": "heartbeat_expiry", "reason": "result_file_present"},
   570→            )
   571→            await self.handle_agent_complete(agent, file_result=file_result)
   572→            return StalledSessionCheckResult.STOP_MONITORING
   573→
   574→        try:
   575→            # Query backend for actual session status
   576→            status = await self.backend.get_session_status(agent.session_id, directory=agent.worktree)
   577→            status_type = status.get("type") if isinstance(status, dict) else None
   578→
   579→            if status_type == "idle":
   580→                # Idle is only a hint; completion still routes through standard handler.
   581→                self.db.log_event(
   582→                    agent.issue_id,
   583→                    agent.agent_id,
   584→                    "missed_completion",
   585→                    {"source": "heartbeat_expiry", "session_status": "idle"},
   586→                )
   587→                await self.handle_agent_complete(agent)
   588→                return StalledSessionCheckResult.STOP_MONITORING
   589→
   590→            if status_type == "busy":
   591→                self._session_last_activity[agent.session_id] = datetime.now()
   592→                self.db.try_touch_agent_heartbeat(agent.agent_id)
   593→                self.db.log_event(
   594→                    agent.issue_id,
   595→                    agent.agent_id,
   596→                    "heartbeat_refreshed",
   597→                    {"session_status": "busy"},
   598→                )
   599→                return StalledSessionCheckResult.CONTINUE_MONITORING
   600→
   601→            if status_type in ("error", "not_found"):
   602→                await self.handle_stalled_agent(agent)
   603→                return StalledSessionCheckResult.STOP_MONITORING
   604→
   605→        except Exception as e:
   606→            # Backend API failure falls through to stalled path.
   607→            self.db.log_event(agent.issue_id, agent.agent_id, "session_check_failed", {"error": str(e), "fallback": "handle_stalled_agent"})
   608→
   609→        # Default fallback behavior
   610→        await self.handle_stalled_agent(agent)
   611→        return StalledSessionCheckResult.STOP_MONITORING
   612→

1→"""Notes mixin: note CRUD."""
     2→
     3→import logging
     4→
     5→from typing import Dict, List, Optional
     6→
     7→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
     8→
     9→
    10→class NotesMixin:
    11→    def add_note(
    12→        self,
    13→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
    14→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
    15→        content: str = "",
    16→        category: str = "discovery",
    17→        project: Optional[str] = None,
    18→        must_read: bool = False,
    19→    ) -> int:
    20→        """
    21→        Insert a note and return its ID.
    22→
    23→        Args:
    24→            issue_id: Which issue the note was discovered during. None = project-wide note.
    25→            agent_id: Which agent wrote it. None = Queen-authored or system note.
    26→            content: The note text. Short — typically 1-3 sentences.
    27→            category: One of 'discovery', 'gotcha', 'dependency', 'pattern', 'context'.
    28→            project: Project identifier. If None and issue_id provided, backfilled via migration.
    29→            must_read: If True, recipients must acknowledge this note before proceeding.
    30→
    31→        Returns:
    32→            The ID of the inserted note
    33→        """
    34→        if not self.conn:
    35→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
    36→
    37→        cursor = self.conn.execute(
    38→            "INSERT INTO notes (issue_id, agent_id, category, content, project, must_read) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
    39→            (issue_id, agent_id, category, content, project, 1 if must_read else 0),
    40→        )
    41→        self.conn.commit()
    42→        return cursor.lastrowid
    43→
    44→    def get_notes(
    45→        self, issue_id: Optional[str] = None, category: Optional[str] = None, project: Optional[str] = None, limit: int = 20
    46→    ) -> List[Dict]:
    47→        """
    48→        Retrieve notes with optional filtering. Returns newest first.
    49→
    50→        Args:
    51→            issue_id: Filter by specific issue ID (optional)
    52→            category: Filter by specific category (optional)
    53→            project: Filter by project (optional). NULL-project notes match any query for backward compat.
    54→            limit: Maximum number of notes to return
    55→
    56→        Returns:
    57→            List of note dicts, ordered by newest first
    58→        """
    59→        if not self.conn:
    60→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
    61→
    62→        query = "SELECT * FROM notes WHERE 1=1"
    63→        params = []
    64→        if issue_id is not None:
    65→            query += " AND issue_id = ?"
    66→            params.append(issue_id)
    67→        if category is not None:
    68→            query += " AND category = ?"
    69→            params.append(category)
    70→        if project is not None:
    71→            query += " AND (project = ? OR project IS NULL)"
    72→            params.append(project)
    73→        query += " ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?"
    74→        params.append(limit)
    75→        rows = self.conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()
    76→        return [dict(row) for row in rows]
    77→

    13→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    14→
    15→
    16→# Allowed tag values. Validated at creation time but stored as free-form JSON
    17→# so the set can grow without a migration.
    18→ALLOWED_TAGS = {
    19→    # Task type
    20→    "refactor",
    21→    "bugfix",
    22→    "feature",
    23→    "test",
    24→    "docs",
    25→    "cleanup",
    26→    "config",
    27→    # Language
    28→    "python",
    29→    "typescript",
    30→    "javascript",
    31→    "sql",
    32→    "shell",
    33→    "markdown",
    34→    # Complexity
    35→    "small",
    36→    "medium",
    37→    "large",
    38→}
    39→
    40→
    41→def validate_tags(tags: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    42→    """Validate and normalize tags. Raises ValueError for unknown tags."""
    43→    normalized = [t.lower().strip() for t in tags]
    44→    unknown = set(normalized) - ALLOWED_TAGS
    45→    if unknown:
    46→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown tags: {unknown}. Allowed: {sorted(ALLOWED_TAGS)}")
    47→    return sorted(set(normalized))  # dedupe and sort for consistency
    48→
    49→
    50→# SQL schema definition
    51→SCHEMA = """
    52→-- WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes
    53→PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
    54→PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
    55→-- NOTE: Existing databases may still have agent_id FK constraints in events, notes, merge_queue.
    56→-- Deletion code must use PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF when deleting agents for backward compatibility.
    57→PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;
    58→
    59→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    60→-- ISSUES: the universal work unit
    61→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues (
    63→    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    64→    title       TEXT NOT NULL,
    65→    description TEXT,
    66→    status      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',
    67→    priority    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2,
    68→    type        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'task',
    69→    assignee    TEXT,
    70→    parent_id   TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
    71→    project     TEXT,
    72→    model       TEXT,
    73→    tags        TEXT,  -- JSON array of tag strings
    74→    metadata    TEXT,
    75→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    76→    updated_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    77→    closed_at   TEXT
    78→);
    79→
    80→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_status ON issues(status);
    81→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_assignee ON issues(assignee);
    82→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_parent ON issues(parent_id);
    83→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_project ON issues(project);
    84→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    85→-- DEPENDENCIES: edges in the work DAG
    86→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    87→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dependencies (
    88→    issue_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id),
    89→    depends_on  TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id),
    90→    type        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'blocks',
    91→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    92→    PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, depends_on)
    93→);
    94→
    95→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    96→-- AGENTS: ephemeral execution identity (deleted after merge/cleanup)
    97→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    98→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agents (
    99→    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   100→    name        TEXT NOT NULL,
   101→    status      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'idle',
   102→    session_id  TEXT,
   103→    worktree    TEXT,
   104→    current_issue TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
   105→    project     TEXT,
   106→    model       TEXT,
   107→    lease_expires_at TEXT,
   108→    last_progress_at TEXT,
   109→    last_heartbeat_at TEXT,
   110→    metadata    TEXT,
   111→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   112→    updated_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   113→);
   114→
   115→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   116→-- EVENTS: append-only audit trail
   117→-- agent_id is a correlation key, not a live FK (agents are deleted after merge)
   118→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   119→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (
   120→    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   121→    issue_id    TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
   122→    agent_id    TEXT,
   123→    event_type  TEXT NOT NULL,
   124→    detail      TEXT,
   125→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   126→);
   127→
   128→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_issue ON events(issue_id);
   129→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_agent ON events(agent_id);
   130→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(event_type);
   131→
   132→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   133→-- NOTES: inter-agent knowledge transfer system
   134→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   135→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notes (
   136→    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   137→    issue_id    TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
   138→    agent_id    TEXT,
   139→    project     TEXT,
   140→    category    TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'discovery',
   141→    content     TEXT NOT NULL,
   142→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   143→);
   144→
   145→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_issue ON notes(issue_id);
   146→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_category ON notes(category);
   147→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_created ON notes(created_at);
   148→
   149→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   150→-- NOTE_DELIVERIES: delivery tracking for notes
   151→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   152→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS note_deliveries (
   153→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   154→    note_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES notes(id),
   155→    recipient_agent_id TEXT,
   156→    recipient_issue_id TEXT,
   157→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued',
   158→    delivered_at TEXT,
   159→    read_at TEXT,
   160→    acked_at TEXT,
   161→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   162→);
   163→
   164→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_deliveries_note ON note_deliveries(note_id);
   165→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_deliveries_inbox ON note_deliveries(recipient_agent_id, recipient_issue_id, status, created_at);
   166→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_note_deliveries_note_agent_global ON note_deliveries(note_id, recipient_agent_id) WHERE recipient_issue_id IS NULL;
   167→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_note_deliveries_note_agent_issue ON note_deliveries(note_id, recipient_agent_id, recipient_issue_id) WHERE recipient_issue_id IS NOT NULL;
   168→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_note_deliveries_note_issue_target ON note_deliveries(note_id, recipient_issue_id) WHERE recipient_agent_id IS NULL;
   169→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_deliveries_issue_targets ON note_deliveries(recipient_issue_id) WHERE recipient_agent_id IS NULL;
   170→
   171→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   172→-- MERGE_QUEUE: dedicated finalizer queue
   173→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   174→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS merge_queue (
   175→    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   176→    issue_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id),
   177→    agent_id    TEXT,
   178→    project     TEXT NOT NULL,
   179→    worktree    TEXT NOT NULL,
   180→    branch_name TEXT NOT NULL,
   181→    test_command TEXT,
   182→    status      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued',
   183→    enqueued_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   184→    completed_at TEXT
   185→);
   186→
   187→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mq_status ON merge_queue(status);
   188→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mq_project ON merge_queue(project);
   189→
   190→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   191→-- PROJECTS: registry of all known projects and their disk paths
   192→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   193→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projects (
   194→    name        TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   195→    path        TEXT NOT NULL,
   196→    registered_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', 'now'))
   197→);
   198→
